examples.title: Algorithm: Image Degradation Model
examples.description: Demonstrates the mathematical model for image degradation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Image Degradation Model
INPUT: f(x, y) (original image), h(x, y) (degradation function), η(x, y) (additive noise)
OUTPUT: g(x, y) (degraded image)

BEGIN
  g(x, y) = h(x, y) ∗ f(x, y) + η(x, y)
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the mathematical model for image degradation, where the degraded image g(x, y) is obtained by convolving the original image f(x, y) with the degradation function h(x, y) and adding the additive noise η(x, y).

examples.title: Python: Image Degradation Model
examples.description: Implements the mathematical model for image degradation using Python
examples.code:
import numpy as np
from scipy.signal import convolve2d

# Define the degradation function (e.g., a 3x3 blur kernel)
h = np.array([[1/9, 1/9, 1/9], [1/9, 1/9, 1/9], [1/9, 1/9, 1/9]])

# Define the original image (e.g., a 256x256 image)
f = np.random.rand(256, 256)

# Define the additive noise (e.g., Gaussian noise with mean 0 and variance 0.01)
eta = np.random.normal(0, 0.01, (256, 256))

g = convolve2d(f, h, mode='same') + eta
examples.explanation: This Python code implements the mathematical model for image degradation by convolving the original image f with the degradation function h and adding the additive noise η.
